messages.content: Find all the roots of a quadratic equation given coefficients a = 3, b = -11, and c = -4.

function.name: solve_quadratic
function.description: Solve a quadratic equation given coefficients a, b, and c. If optional 'root_type' is 'real', the function will only return real roots. If not specified, function may return complex roots. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

a.description: The coefficient of the squared term in the quadratic equation.

b.description: The coefficient of the linear term in the quadratic equation.

c.description: The constant term in the quadratic equation.

root_type.description: The type of roots to return: 'real' for real roots, 'all' for both real and complex roots. Default value is 'real'.

messages.content: Solve a quadratic equation where a=2, b=6, and c=5

function.name: solve_quadratic_equation
function.description: Function solves the quadratic equation and returns its roots. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

c.description: Constant term in the quadratic equation.

messages.content: Find the roots of a quadratic equation with coefficients a=1, b=-3, c=2.

function.name: algebra_quadratic_roots
function.description: Find the roots of a quadratic equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

messages.content: Calculate the hypotenuse of a right triangle given the lengths of the other two sides as 4 and 5.

function.name: math_hypot
function.description: Calculate the Euclidean norm, sqrt(sum(squares)), the length of the vector from the origin to point (x, y) which is the hypotenuse of the right triangle. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

z.description: Optional. The z-coordinate value. Default is 0.

messages.content: Calculate the factorial of 5 using math functions.

function.name: math_factorial
function.description: Calculate the factorial of a given number. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

number.description: The number for which factorial needs to be calculated.

messages.content: What is the final velocity of a vehicle that started from rest and accelerated at 4 m/s^2 for a distance of 300 meters?

function.name: kinematics_final_velocity_from_distance
function.description: Calculate the final velocity of an object given the acceleration and distance travelled, assuming initial velocity is 0. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

distance.description: Distance traveled by the object, m.

initial_velocity.description: Initial velocity of the object. Default is 0, m/s This is a float type value.

messages.content: Calculate the displacement of a car given the initial velocity of 10 and acceleeration of 9.8 within 5 seconds.

function.name: calculate_displacement
function.description: Calculates the displacement of an object in motion given initial velocity, time, and acceleration. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

initial_velocity.description: The initial velocity of the object in m/s.

time.description: The time in seconds that the object has been in motion.

acceleration.description: The acceleration of the object in m/s^2. This is a float type value.

messages.content: Find the greatest common divisor (GCD) of 12 and 18

function.name: math_gcd
function.description: Calculate the greatest common divisor of two integers. Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.
